adjusted.reasoning: Croatia (3pts) must win to be sure of going through; Ghana (4pts) advance with a draw and have built their group on defending — two clean sheets under Queiroz — so they will sit deep for the point that qualifies them. Croatia have looked laboured and over-reliant on a 40-year-old Modric. Market makes Croatia a modest favourite (-131, ~54%) with a very live draw (~29%); I nudge the draw up a touch for Ghana's game plan. Model's 95% Croatia is wildly overconfident. Low-scoring (under 2.5).

factors.factor: CRO must win; GHA (4pts) advance on a draw and defend deep (2 clean sheets); under 2.5
Croatia -131, Kalshi CRO 55 / draw 29 / GHA 17; CRO laboured, reliant on Modric

odds.source: Kalshi/covers 2026-06-27 (CRO -131; CRO 55/draw 29/GHA 17); CRO must win, GHA through on draw, under2.5

adjusted.reasoning: Both already qualified: Colombia (6pts) take top with a draw, Portugal (4pts) must win to claim first and a kinder draw. Market makes Portugal a slight favourite (-115, ~51%) on their greater attacking quality (Ronaldo, Joao Neves, Leao); I give Colombia a little back for a Miami pseudo-home crowd and two wins from two. Several previews call it a cagey 1-1. Sits between the market (POR-leaning) and the model (which over-favours Colombia at home).

adjusted.reasoning: Runner-up decider: Austria (2nd, GD 0) advance with a draw or win, Algeria (3rd, GD -2) must win to be certain. Austria are content to play for the point that qualifies them, so the market prices a very high draw (~43%, draw just +120) — and Austria's squad depth (Sabitzer, Laimer, Alaba) edges the win market over Algeria. The model badly underweights the draw (24%); I anchor to the live market, mirroring the qualification dynamic that produced the Paraguay-Australia 0-0.

factors.factor: Draw sends Austria through on GD & eliminates Algeria; ALG must win
